message.content: Adversaries may acquire and configure serverless cloud infrastructure, such as Cloudflare Workers or AWS Lambda functions, to support their operations. This technique complicates attribution, as the serverless environment can be used to either directly interact with infected machines or proxy traffic to an adversary-controlled command and control server. The use of serverless infrastructure makes malicious traffic appear as legitimate traffic from common cloud providers, thereby evading detection. Common exploitation methods include setting up serverless functions to handle command and control communications or to serve as intermediaries for malicious activities. Mitigation strategies involve monitoring and analyzing traffic patterns to cloud providers, implementing strict access controls, and employing anomaly detection systems to identify unusual behavior associated with serverless functions.
